testcase.name: test_namespace_setup

error.message: failed on setup with "requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)"

testcase.error:
self = , kwargs = {} def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any. :param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """ if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) try: > return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\site-packages\requests\models.py:974: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\__init__.py:346: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\decoder.py:344: in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ self = s = '\n\n500 Internal Server Error\n

Internal Server Error

\n

The ser...nd was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

\n' idx = 0 def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended. This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end. """ try: obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx) except StopIteration as err: > raise JSONDecodeError("Expecting value", s, err.value) from None E json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\decoder.py:362: JSONDecodeError During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def setup_ipsec(): # Idempotent setup endpoint > resp = post("/api/ipsec/setup", {}) conftest.py:8: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ test_lib\api.py:24: in post return r.json() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ self = , kwargs = {} def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any. :param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """ if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) try: return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) except JSONDecodeError as e: # Catch JSON-related errors and raise as requests.JSONDecodeError # This aliases json.JSONDecodeError and simplejson.JSONDecodeError > raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) E requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\site-packages\requests\models.py:978: JSONDecodeError

testcase.name: test_sa_stats

failure.message: requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 400 Client Error: BAD REQUEST for url: http://192.168.0.21:8080/api/ipsec/stats?ns=hostA

testcase.name: test_iperf_traffic
test_sa_stats

failure.message: requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 400 Client Error: BAD REQUEST for url: http://192.168.0.21:8080/api/ipsec/stats?ns=hostB

testcase.name: test_ipsec_cleanup

error.message: failed on setup with "requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)"

testcase.error:
self = , kwargs = {} def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any. :param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """ if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) try: > return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\site-packages\requests\models.py:974: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\__init__.py:346: in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\decoder.py:344: in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ self = s = '\n\n500 Internal Server Error\n

\n

The ser...nd was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

\n' idx = 0 def raw_decode(self, s, idx=0): """Decode a JSON document from ``s`` (a ``str`` beginning with a JSON document) and return a 2-tuple of the Python representation and the index in ``s`` where the document ended. This can be used to decode a JSON document from a string that may have extraneous data at the end. """ try: obj, end = self.scan_once(s, idx) except StopIteration as err: > raise JSONDecodeError("Expecting value", s, err.value) from None E json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\json\decoder.py:362: JSONDecodeError During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred: @pytest.fixture(scope="session") def setup_ipsec(): # Idempotent setup endpoint > resp = post("/api/ipsec/setup", {}) conftest.py:8: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ test_lib\api.py:24: in post return r.json() _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ self = , kwargs = {} def json(self, **kwargs): r"""Returns the json-encoded content of a response, if any. :param \*\*kwargs: Optional arguments that ``json.loads`` takes. :raises requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: If the response body does not contain valid json. """ if not self.encoding and self.content and len(self.content) > 3: # No encoding set. JSON RFC 4627 section 3 states we should expect # UTF-8, -16 or -32. Detect which one to use; If the detection or # decoding fails, fall back to `self.text` (using charset_normalizer to make # a best guess). encoding = guess_json_utf(self.content) if encoding is not None: try: return complexjson.loads(self.content.decode(encoding), **kwargs) except UnicodeDecodeError: # Wrong UTF codec detected; usually because it's not UTF-8 # but some other 8-bit codec. This is an RFC violation, # and the server didn't bother to tell us what codec *was* # used. pass except JSONDecodeError as e: raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) try: return complexjson.loads(self.text, **kwargs) except JSONDecodeError as e: # Catch JSON-related errors and raise as requests.JSONDecodeError # This aliases json.JSONDecodeError and simplejson.JSONDecodeError > raise RequestsJSONDecodeError(e.msg, e.doc, e.pos) E requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) C:\Users\prash\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python313\Lib\site-packages\requests\models.py:978: JSONDecodeError
